items.title: Insurer Elevance Seeks Recalculated Medicare Star Rating in Lawsuit - Benzinga
items.reason: Elevance (formerly Anthem) is filing litigation challenging a Medicare Star Rating calculation, which is a direct regulatory enforcement matter affecting a major payer's compliance and financial performance under Medicare Advantage programs.
items.summary: Elevance Health has filed a lawsuit seeking recalculation of its Medicare Star Rating, a key performance metric used by CMS to evaluate Medicare Advantage plan quality and determine bonus payments and penalties. Star Ratings directly impact plan competitiveness, member enrollment, and revenue; disputes over rating methodology can result in significant financial consequences for insurers. This litigation represents a payer challenge to CMS regulatory determinations and may affect how Star Rating calculations are applied industry-wide. Revenue-cycle teams should monitor the outcome as it may influence Medicare Advantage reimbursement dynamics and payer financial performance going forward.
